title: What Would Overflow as My Natural State Look Like?
subtitle: Journal prompts for picturing what daily life looks like when wealth is a constant
excerpt: Five journal prompts for sitting with overflow as a daily reality rather than an occasional windfall. The prompts move from how overflow would change behaviour and decisions to what evidence already exists in ordinary life that abundance keeps replenishing.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 57 reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: Five journal prompts for making overflow concrete. The question 'if I truly believed wealth could never run out' stays abstract until you write the specific decisions, words, and daily behaviours that would change. These prompts do that narrowing. Set twenty minutes and write without stopping.
New to journalling as practice? Read
If I truly believed wealth could never run out, how would I show up differently today?
Write one specific decision, one specific word you would use, one thing you would stop second-guessing.
Where in my life has abundance already kept replenishing?
Look for examples, large or small. Income arriving when you needed it. An idea that came at the right time. Write three.
What decisions do I currently make from scarcity that I would make differently from overflow?
Name a specific decision and the two versions: how you make it now and how you would make it if abundance was certain.
What emotions or behaviours would I embody if overflow were my daily normal?
Write as a list. Not aspirations, behaviours. 'I would call back immediately' or 'I would buy the good coffee without calculating.'
What is one small way I can act from overflow today?
Name something specific and do-able before the day ends. Not a grand gesture, something you currently second-guess.
Adapted from the Day 57 reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
